251.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Hazzal from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he mentioned something of the affair of Ma'iz to the Prophet ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'Would that you had left him alone. Perhaps he would have repented and Allah would have accepted his repentance.'
251.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Hazzal dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menyebutkan sesuatu dari perkara Ma'iz kepada Nabi ﷺ. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Seandainya kamu membiarkannya saja. Barangkali ia akan bertaubat dan Allah akan menerima taubatnya.'

252.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Hazzal from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'Woe to you, Nu'aym! Would that you had covered him with your garment — that would have been better for you.'
252.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Hazzal dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Celaka kamu, Nu'aym! Seandainya kamu menutupinya dengan pakaianmu — itu lebih baik bagimu.'

253.iwh_en: It was narrated that Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) asked Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him): 'What did the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recite in the prayer on the Day of Eid al-Adha and Eid al-Fitr?' He said: 'He recited Qaf and Al-Qamar.'
253.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bertanya kepada Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu): 'Apa yang dibaca Rasulullah ﷺ dalam shalat pada Hari Raya Idul Adha dan Idul Fitri?' Ia berkata: 'Beliau membaca Qaf dan Al-Qamar.'

254.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that they set out from Mecca with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ toward Hunayn. They passed a lote tree and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, make for us a Dhat Anwat as they have a Dhat Anwat.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allahu Akbar! By the One in whose hand is my soul, you have said what the people of Musa said: «Make for us a god as they have gods.» [Quran 7:138] This is the way of those before you.'
254.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa mereka keluar dari Mekah bersama Rasulullah ﷺ menuju Hunain. Mereka melewati sebuah pohon bidara dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, jadikanlah untuk kami Dhat Anwat seperti mereka memiliki Dhat Anwat.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allahu Akbar! Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, kalian telah mengucapkan seperti apa yang diucapkan kaum Musa: «Jadikanlah untuk kami tuhan seperti tuhan-tuhan mereka.» [QS 7:138] Inilah jalan orang-orang sebelum kalian.'

255.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah, we are in a land where famine strikes us — what is permissible for us from carrion?' He said: 'When you have not satisfied your hunger with something else, and you find no herbs or vegetables, then you may eat of it.'
255.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, kami berada di tanah yang ditimpa kelaparan — apa yang halal bagi kami dari bangkai?' Beliau bersabda: 'Jika kamu belum kenyang dengan yang lain, dan kamu tidak menemukan tumbuhan atau sayuran, maka kamu boleh memakannya.'

256.iwh_en: It was narrated from Nafi' ibn Sargis that they visited Abu Waqid al-Badri in his illness from which he died. He heard Abu Waqid say: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite in the two Eid prayers Qaf and Iqtarabat al-Sa'ah.'
256.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Nafi' ibn Sargis bahwa mereka mengunjungi Abu Waqid al-Badri saat ia sakit keras hingga wafatnya. Ia mendengar Abu Waqid berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca dalam dua shalat Ied: Qaf dan Iqtarabat al-Sa'ah (Al-Qamar).'

257.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We went out with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ toward Hunayn and we passed by a lote tree. Some of the men said: «O Messenger of Allah, make for us a Dhat Anwat like theirs.» The Prophet ﷺ said: «Allahu Akbar! These are the ways. By the One in whose hand is my soul, you said what the Children of Israel said to Musa: «Make for us a god as they have gods.» I swear you will follow the ways of those before you.»'
257.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami keluar bersama Rasulullah ﷺ menuju Hunain dan kami melewati sebuah pohon bidara. Sebagian laki-laki berkata: «Wahai Rasulullah, jadikanlah untuk kami Dhat Anwat seperti milik mereka.» Nabi ﷺ bersabda: «Allahu Akbar! Inilah jalan-jalan itu. Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, kalian mengucapkan seperti yang diucapkan Bani Israil kepada Musa: «Jadikanlah untuk kami tuhan seperti tuhan-tuhan mereka.» Sungguh kalian akan mengikuti jalan orang-orang sebelum kalian.»'

258.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that they said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are in a land where famine strikes us — when is carrion permissible for us?' He said: 'If you have not satisfied your morning or evening hunger and find nothing else, then it is permissible.'
258.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa mereka berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, kami berada di tanah yang ditimpa kelaparan — kapan bangkai halal bagi kami?' Beliau bersabda: 'Jika kamu belum kenyang pagi atau sorenya dan tidak menemukan yang lain, maka ia halal.'

259.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whatever is cut from a living animal is dead flesh.'
259.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apa yang dipotong dari hewan yang masih hidup adalah bangkai.'

260.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ arrived in Medina, the people had camels and sheep and they would cut their humps and fat tails while the animals were alive. The Prophet ﷺ said: «Whatever is cut from a living animal is dead flesh.»'
260.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, orang-orang memiliki unta dan kambing dan mereka memotong punuk dan ekor gemuknya saat hewan masih hidup. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: «Apa yang dipotong dari hewan yang masih hidup adalah bangkai.»'

261.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said to his wives in his Farewell Pilgrimage: 'This is the last, then you must commit to sitting in your homes.'
261.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada istri-istrinya dalam Haji Wada': 'Ini adalah yang terakhir, kemudian kalian harus duduk di rumah-rumah kalian.'

262.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We used to come to the Prophet ﷺ when a revelation descended upon him and he would narrate to us. He said: «Faith begins as something strange and will return to being strange, so glad tidings to the strangers who correct what the people have corrupted after me.»'
262.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami biasa datang kepada Nabi ﷺ ketika wahyu turun kepada beliau dan beliau menceritakan kepada kami. Beliau bersabda: «Iman dimulai sebagai sesuatu yang asing dan akan kembali menjadi asing, maka berbahagialah bagi orang-orang yang asing yang memperbaiki apa yang dirusak manusia setelahku.»'

263.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he had three men with him. He said: «Whoever takes care of three daughters or three sisters, teaches them good manners, shows them mercy and provides for them — for him is Paradise.»'
263.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ sementara ada tiga orang bersamanya. Beliau bersabda: «Barangsiapa merawat tiga anak perempuan atau tiga saudari perempuan, mendidik mereka dengan baik, menyayangi mereka dan memberi nafkah kepada mereka — baginya adalah surga.»'

264.iwh_en: It was narrated from Nafi' ibn Sargis that he visited Abu Waqid al-Kindi during his final illness and heard him say: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite in the two Eid prayers: Qaf and Iqtarabat al-Sa'ah.'
264.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Nafi' ibn Sargis bahwa ia mengunjungi Abu Waqid al-Kindi saat sakit terakhirnya dan mendengarnya berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca dalam dua shalat Ied: Qaf dan Iqtarabat al-Sa'ah.'

265.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said to his wives during his Farewell Pilgrimage: 'After this you must remain in your homes.'
265.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada istri-istrinya saat Haji Wada': 'Setelah ini kalian harus tetap di rumah-rumah kalian.'

266.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) asked me what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited in the prayers of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. I told him: He recited Qaf and Iqtarabat.'
266.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laytsi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bertanya kepadaku apa yang dibaca Rasulullah ﷺ dalam shalat Idul Fitri dan Idul Adha. Aku memberitahunya: Beliau membaca Qaf dan Iqtarabat.'

267.iwh_en: It was narrated that Nafi' ibn Sargis visited Abu Waqid al-Laythi — the Companion of the Prophet ﷺ — in his final illness. He heard Abu Waqid say: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite in the two Eid prayers Qaf and Al-Qamar, from beginning to end in each.'
267.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nafi' ibn Sargis mengunjungi Abu Waqid al-Laytsi — sahabat Nabi ﷺ — saat sakit terakhirnya. Ia mendengar Abu Waqid berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca dalam dua shalat Ied: Qaf dan Al-Qamar, dari awal hingga akhir pada masing-masing.'

268.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'Whoever keeps a dog that does not serve for farming or herding will lose one qirat of his reward each day.'
268.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memelihara anjing yang tidak digunakan untuk pertanian atau penggembalaan, akan berkurang satu qirat dari pahalanya setiap hari.'

269.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever keeps a dog that is not used for hunting, herding livestock, or farming will lose a qirat from his good deeds every day.'
269.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memelihara anjing yang tidak digunakan untuk berburu, menjaga ternak, atau bertani, akan berkurang satu qirat dari amal baiknya setiap hari.'

270.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr al-Bahzi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «Whoever keeps a dog that does not protect his crops or cattle will lose two qirats from his reward every day.»'
270.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr al-Bahzi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Barangsiapa memelihara anjing yang tidak menjaga tanaman atau ternaknya, akan berkurang dua qirat dari pahalanya setiap hari.»'

271.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (may Allah be pleased with him) — a man from Shanu'a and one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ — that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «Whoever keeps a dog that does not guard his crops or cattle will lose a qirat from his reward each day.»'
271.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — seorang dari Shanu'a dan salah satu sahabat Nabi ﷺ — bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Barangsiapa memelihara anjing yang tidak menjaga tanaman atau ternaknya, akan berkurang satu qirat dari pahalanya setiap hari.»'

272.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «The Caliphate will last thirty years, then kingship after that.»' Safina then said: 'Count the caliphate of Abu Bakr two years, then Umar ten years, then Uthman twelve years, then Ali six years.'
272.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Khilafah akan berlangsung tiga puluh tahun, kemudian kerajaan setelah itu.»' Safinah kemudian berkata: 'Hitunglah kekhalifahan Abu Bakr dua tahun, kemudian Umar sepuluh tahun, kemudian Utsman dua belas tahun, kemudian Ali enam tahun.'

273.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man struck his she-camel with a stick until it died. He asked the Prophet ﷺ about it and he commanded them to eat it.
273.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki memukul untanya dengan sebuah batang kayu hingga mati. Ia bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang hal itu dan beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk memakannya.'

274.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was carrying a heavy load, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'You are Safina — a ship.'
274.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia membawa beban yang sangat berat, dan Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Kamu adalah Safinah — sebuah kapal.'

275.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man was a guest of Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him), so they prepared food for him. Fatima (may Allah be pleased with her) said: 'If only we had invited the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to eat with us.' So they invited him and he came. The Prophet ﷺ placed his hand on the doorframe and saw the curtain. He turned and left. Fatima said: 'O Safina, follow the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and see what happened.' I followed him and he said: 'O Safina, it is not for me — or he said, it was not for a prophet — to enter a house that is decorated.'
275.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertamu kepada Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dan mereka menyiapkan makanan untuknya. Fatimah (radhiyallahu 'anha) berkata: 'Seandainya kita mengundang Rasulullah ﷺ untuk makan bersama kita.' Maka mereka mengundangnya dan beliau datang. Nabi ﷺ meletakkan tangannya di kusen pintu dan melihat tirai. Beliau berbalik dan pergi. Fatimah berkata: 'Wahai Safinah, ikutilah Rasulullah ﷺ dan lihatlah apa yang terjadi.' Aku mengikutinya dan beliau berkata: 'Wahai Safinah, tidaklah pantas bagiku — atau beliau berkata: tidak pantas bagi seorang nabi — untuk masuk ke rumah yang dihias.'

276.iwh_en: It was narrated from a mawla of Umm Salama that he said: 'I was with the Prophet ﷺ on a journey and we came to a valley. I began ferrying people across or carrying them. The Prophet ﷺ said to me: «You are Safina.»'
276.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang mawla Umm Salamah bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bersama Nabi ﷺ dalam sebuah perjalanan dan kami tiba di sebuah lembah. Aku mulai menyeberangkan orang-orang atau membawa mereka. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadaku: «Kamu adalah Safinah.»'

277.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a journey. Whenever someone grew tired, he would hand me his sword, his shield, and his spear until I was carrying a great amount of that. The Prophet ﷺ said: «You are Safina.»'
277.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam sebuah perjalanan. Setiap kali seseorang kelelahan, ia memberikan kepadaku pedangnya, perisainya, dan tombaknya hingga aku membawa banyak sekali beban itu. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: «Kamu adalah Safinah.»'

278.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina Abu Abd al-Rahman (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man was a guest of Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him). Ali prepared food for him. Fatima said: 'If only we invited the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to eat with us.' So they sent for him and he came. When he reached the doorway he saw a decorated curtain, so he turned and did not enter. Safina said: 'I followed him and informed Ali and Fatima. Ali came out and called to him, so he entered and ate with them.'
278.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah Abu Abd al-Rahman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertamu kepada Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu). Ali menyiapkan makanan untuknya. Fatimah berkata: 'Seandainya kita mengundang Rasulullah ﷺ untuk makan bersama kita.' Maka mereka mengutus seseorang menjemputnya dan beliau datang. Ketika beliau sampai di pintu masuk, beliau melihat tirai yang berhias, maka beliau berbalik dan tidak masuk. Safinah berkata: 'Aku mengikutinya dan memberitahu Ali dan Fatimah. Ali keluar dan memanggilnya, maka beliau masuk dan makan bersama mereka.'

279.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina Abu Abd al-Rahman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Umm Salama freed me and stipulated that I must serve the Prophet ﷺ for as long as he lived.'
279.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah Abu Abd al-Rahman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Umm Salamah memerdekakanku dengan syarat aku harus melayani Nabi ﷺ selama beliau hidup.'

280.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Caliphate in my nation will last thirty years, then kingship after that.' Safina then said: 'Count: Abu Bakr's caliphate, then Umar's caliphate, then Uthman's caliphate, then Ali's caliphate — and we found them to be thirty years.'
280.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Khilafah dalam umatku akan berlangsung tiga puluh tahun, kemudian kerajaan setelah itu.' Safinah kemudian berkata: 'Hitunglah: kekhalifahan Abu Bakr, kemudian kekhalifahan Umar, kemudian kekhalifahan Utsman, kemudian kekhalifahan Ali — dan kami mendapatinya tiga puluh tahun.'

281.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina mawla of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon to us and said: «Know that there was no prophet before me except that he had followers and companions who took to his sunnah and followed his command. Then after them came successors who said what they did not do and did what they were not commanded. So whoever strives against them with his hand is a believer, and whoever strives against them with his tongue is a believer, and whoever strives against them with his heart is a believer. There is not a grain of faith beyond that.»'
281.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah mawla Rasulullah ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah kepada kami dan bersabda: «Ketahuilah bahwa tidak ada nabi sebelumku kecuali ia memiliki pengikut dan sahabat yang mengikuti sunnahnya dan menjalankan perintahnya. Kemudian setelah mereka datang penerus yang mengatakan apa yang tidak mereka kerjakan dan mengerjakan apa yang tidak mereka diperintahkan. Maka barangsiapa berjuang melawan mereka dengan tangannya ia adalah mukmin, dan barangsiapa berjuang dengan lisannya ia adalah mukmin, dan barangsiapa berjuang dengan hatinya ia adalah mukmin. Tidak ada iman seberat biji sawi pun di luar itu.»'

282.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina mawla of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to perform ablution with one mudd of water and bathe with one sa'.
282.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah mawla Rasulullah ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berwudhu dengan satu mudd air dan mandi dengan satu sha'.'

283.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina — the Companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to bathe with a sa' and perform ablution with a mudd.'
283.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah — sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ — (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mandi dengan satu sha' dan berwudhu dengan satu mudd.'

284.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were on a journey. Whenever a man grew tired he would hand me his clothing, a shield, or a sword, until I was carrying a great deal. The Prophet ﷺ said: «You are a ship — Safina.»'
284.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami sedang dalam perjalanan. Setiap kali seorang laki-laki kelelahan, ia memberikan kepadaku pakaiannya, perisai, atau pedang, hingga aku membawa banyak sekali. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: «Kamu adalah kapal — Safinah.»'

285.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safina (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man was a guest of Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) and food was prepared for him. Fatima said to Ali: 'If only you would invite the Prophet ﷺ to eat with us.' So they invited him and he came. When he arrived he saw a decorated curtain and turned away. Safina was sent after him and informed Ali. Ali raised the curtain and the Prophet ﷺ entered and ate with them.
285.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safinah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertamu kepada Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dan makanan disiapkan untuknya. Fatimah berkata kepada Ali: 'Seandainya kamu mengundang Nabi ﷺ untuk makan bersama kita.' Maka mereka mengundangnya dan beliau datang. Ketika tiba, beliau melihat tirai berhias dan berbalik pergi. Safinah dikirim untuk mengikutinya dan memberitahu Ali. Ali membuka tirai itu dan Nabi ﷺ masuk dan makan bersama mereka.'

286.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabala ibn Haritha (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah, teach me something to say before I sleep. He said: «When you lie down to sleep, recite Al-Kafirun — for it is a disavowal of polytheism.»'
286.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabalah ibn Haritsah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, ajari aku sesuatu untuk aku ucapkan sebelum tidur. Beliau bersabda: «Jika kamu hendak tidur, bacalah Al-Kafirun — karena ia adalah berlepas diri dari kemusyrikan.»'

287.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'id ibn Sa'd ibn 'Ubada (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'There was among our dwellings a weak, feeble man. The people of the household did not notice that he had committed fornication with a slave girl of theirs. Sa'd went to the Prophet ﷺ and mentioned it. The Prophet ﷺ said: «Strike him fifty times.»'
287.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'id ibn Sa'd ibn 'Ubadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Di antara rumah-rumah kami ada seorang laki-laki yang lemah dan tidak berdaya. Penghuni rumah tidak menyadari bahwa ia telah berzina dengan seorang budak perempuan mereka. Sa'd pergi kepada Nabi ﷺ dan menyebutkannya. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: «Pukullah ia lima puluh kali.»'

288.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) passed by Hassan while he was reciting poetry in the mosque and glared at him. Hassan said: 'I used to recite here when there was someone better than you present.' Then he turned to Abu Hurayra and said: 'Have you not heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «O Hassan, reply on my behalf. O Allah, support him with the Holy Spirit.»?' Abu Hurayra said: 'Yes.'
288.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melewati Hassan saat ia membacakan syair di masjid dan memelototinya. Hassan berkata: 'Aku biasa membacakan di sini ketika ada seseorang yang lebih baik darimu hadir di sini.' Kemudian ia berpaling kepada Abu Hurairah dan berkata: 'Bukankah kamu telah mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Wahai Hassan, balaslah demi membelaku. Ya Allah, dukunglah ia dengan Ruh al-Qudus.»?' Abu Hurairah berkata: 'Ya.'

289.iwh_en: It was narrated that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) passed by Hassan while he was reciting poetry in the mosque. He said: 'In the mosque of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ you recite poetry?' Hassan replied: 'I recited here for someone better than you, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to listen.'
289.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melewati Hassan saat ia membacakan syair di masjid. Ia berkata: 'Di masjid Rasulullah ﷺ kamu membacakan syair?' Hassan menjawab: 'Aku membacakan di sini untuk seseorang yang lebih baik darimu, dan Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mendengarkan.'

290.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib that Umar passed by Hassan while he was reciting poetry in the mosque. He said: 'Stop.' Hassan replied: 'I recited here for someone better than you.' Umar then passed on.
290.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib bahwa Umar melewati Hassan saat ia membacakan syair di masjid. Ia berkata: 'Berhenti.' Hassan menjawab: 'Aku membacakan di sini untuk seseorang yang lebih baik darimu.' Kemudian Umar berlalu.'

291.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn al-Musayyib that Hassan ibn Thabit was reciting poetry in the mosque and Umar passed by him and glared at him. Hassan said: 'By Allah, I recited here in the presence of one far better than you.' He was afraid Umar would harm him, so he looked at Abu Hurayra for help. Abu Hurayra confirmed that the Prophet ﷺ had said to Hassan: 'Reply on my behalf, O Allah support him with the Holy Spirit.'
291.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn al-Musayyib bahwa Hassan ibn Tsabit sedang membacakan syair di masjid dan Umar melewatinya serta memelototinya. Hassan berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku membacakan di sini di hadapan orang yang jauh lebih baik darimu.' Ia takut Umar akan menyakitinya, maka ia meminta bantuan kepada Abu Hurairah. Abu Hurairah membenarkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ telah berkata kepada Hassan: 'Balaslah demi membelaku, ya Allah dukunglah ia dengan Ruh al-Qudus.'

292.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Hazm (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that he said: 'Do not sit on graves and do not pray toward them.'
292.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Hazm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Janganlah duduk di atas kuburan dan janganlah shalat menghadap ke arahnya.'

293.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Hazm (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw him reclining against a grave and said to him: 'Do not harm the occupant of this grave, and do not let him harm you.'
293.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Hazm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melihatnya bersandar pada sebuah kuburan dan berkata kepadanya: 'Janganlah menyakiti penghuni kuburan ini, dan janganlah ia menyakitimu.'

294.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Hazm (may Allah be pleased with him) that a snakebite was brought before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he commanded that it be treated with ruqya.
294.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Hazm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa sengatan ular diperlihatkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau memerintahkan agar diobati dengan ruqyah.'

295.iwh_en: It was narrated that 'Umara ibn Hazm (may Allah be pleased with him) testified that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw him leaning on a grave and said: 'Do not harm the occupant of the grave.'
295.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa 'Umarah ibn Hazm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bersaksi bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melihatnya bersandar pada sebuah kuburan dan bersabda: 'Janganlah menyakiti penghuni kuburan.'

296.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Hazm — either 'Amr or 'Umara (may Allah be pleased with him) — that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw him reclining against a grave and said: 'Get away from the grave — do not harm its occupant and do not let it harm you.'
296.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Hazm — baik 'Amr maupun 'Umarah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melihatnya bersandar pada sebuah kuburan dan bersabda: 'Menjauhlah dari kuburan — janganlah menyakiti penghuninya dan janganlah ia menyakitimu.'

297.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I witnessed Khaybar with my masters. They spoke to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on my behalf, and he commanded that a sword be hung on me. But when I wore it, it dragged on the ground. He was informed of this and he commanded that it be shortened for me, and he gave me a share of the spoils.'
297.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku menyaksikan Khaibar bersama tuan-tuanku. Mereka berbicara kepada Rasulullah ﷺ untukku, dan beliau memerintahkan agar sebuah pedang digantungkan padaku. Tetapi ketika aku memakainya, pedang itu menyeret di tanah. Beliau diberitahu tentang hal ini dan memerintahkan agar dipendekkan untukku, dan beliau memberiku bagian dari ghanimah.'

298.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I witnessed Khaybar with my masters. I was a slave so they spoke to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on my behalf. He looked at me and gave me a share from the spoils of Khaybar, but not a full share like a free man — a lesser share.'
298.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku menyaksikan Khaibar bersama tuan-tuanku. Aku adalah seorang budak maka mereka berbicara kepada Rasulullah ﷺ untukku. Beliau memandangku dan memberiku bagian dari rampasan Khaibar, tetapi bukan bagian penuh seperti orang merdeka — bagian yang lebih kecil.'

299.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I set out with my masters intending to emigrate until we reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. I was a slave, so I was not given a full share of the spoils. I was given weapons and clothing.'
299.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berangkat bersama tuan-tuanku dengan niat berhijrah hingga kami sampai kepada Rasulullah ﷺ. Aku adalah seorang budak, maka aku tidak diberi bagian penuh dari ghanimah. Aku diberi senjata dan pakaian.'

300.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him) from his mawla 'Umayr that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performing ruqya on a sick person who was brought to him. He was reciting over him and blowing.
300.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari mawlanya 'Umayr bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan ruqyah atas seorang yang sakit yang dibawa kepadanya. Beliau membaca doa atasnya dan meniupkan.'
